competitor_patterns: Extreme word counts: top-ranking pages (Tifosi, Bikerumor) average 20,000–28,000 words — far exceeding our longest post at ~5,163 words. They function as exhaustive category hubs, not single-product articles.

Structured comparison H2s per product: Bikerumor uses a dedicated H2 for every reviewed model (Julbo Density, Tifosi Rail, Oakley Sutro Lite, POC Devour) — giving each product its own indexable section and enabling featured-snippet capture per model name.

Review signals at scale: all top-3 competitors combine has_reviews=true with FAQ sections — creating dual rich-result eligibility (review snippets + FAQ dropdowns) that increases SERP real estate and CTR.

Interchangeable lens content targets its own dedicated URL: Evil Eye ranks #1 for 'interchangeable lens cycling glasses' with a standalone page built entirely around that feature, not embedded inside a broader buying guide.

Lens-type segmentation drives multi-page architecture: competitors separate photochromic, polarised, interchangeable, and shield lens content into distinct indexable pages rather than collapsing them into one guide, multiplying their topical authority signals.
our_gaps: Content depth ceiling at ~5,000 words: our deepest post is 5,163 words versus competitor hubs at 24,000–28,000 words. We lack the comprehensive coverage that earns topical authority signals and featured-snippet wins across multiple sub-questions.

No verified user-review or star-rating markup on any post: competitors combine reviews + FAQ schema for dual rich results; our FAQ schema coverage is only 33% and we have zero review schema, leaving significant SERP real estate unclaimed.

Seventeen posts under 1,100 words with neither a table nor FAQ schema: these thin pages dilute crawl budget and provide no structured-data signals — they are unlikely to rank competitively against 13,000–28,000-word competitor pages for the same keywords.

Missing dedicated interchangeable-lens hub: Evil Eye ranks #1 for that term with a standalone URL. Our interchangeable-lens content is spread across at least four shorter posts (1,088–1,712 words) without a consolidated, authoritative pillar page.

No comparison table on the highest-impression Oakley-alternatives pages: 'best-oakley-alternatives-for-cyclists-2026-tested-ranked' (3,924 words, table=NO) targets the #2 traffic cluster (265 impressions, 'oakley alternatives') yet lacks the side-by-side table format that both users and algorithms reward in comparison queries.
keyword_opportunities: 'photochromic vs interchangeable lens cycling glasses 2026' — competitors cover each type separately but no one owns the direct comparison; our photochromic post (1,104 words) and interchangeable posts are too thin to compete, but a merged 4,000-word versus article with a decision table could own this gap.

'cycling glasses for climbers anti-fog 2026' — Bikerumor and Tifosi mention anti-fog generically; our StradaPro's engineered anti-fog system is a specific technical advantage that maps directly to a high-intent long-tail cluster ('anti-fog cycling glasses climb', 'best glasses for col riding') currently served by thin pages.

'lightweight cycling glasses under 30g 2026' — the 25g frame spec is a concrete, searchable differentiator; no competitor leads with a gram-based weight filter in their titles. A dedicated weight-focused post with a grams comparison table targets gear-obsessed road cyclists who filter by this spec.

'Oakley Radar alternative cycling glasses' — GSC shows 63 impressions at position 4.4 with only 1.6% CTR, meaning we rank well but fail to convert. A post built specifically around the Radar EV Path form factor and how StradaPro compares on weight, ventilation, and price could capture this high-intent model-specific query.

'cycling glasses 30-day trial return policy' — no competitor leads with trial/return policy as a primary content hook. Our 30-day risk-free trial is a unique conversion trigger; a post targeting 'try before you buy cycling glasses' or 'cycling glasses free trial UK/EU' would face near-zero competition and attract bottom-of-funnel buyers.
next_post_guidelines: Target the 'oakley alternatives' cluster first (265 impressions, 2.6% CTR, pos 6.4): write a 4,500+ word definitive guide titled around that exact phrase, include a 6-column comparison table (Oakley / SunGod / Roka / POC / Tifosi / Velluto) with weight, lens system, price, and trial policy rows, and add FAQ schema covering at least 6 questions searchers ask about Oakley alternatives.

Add a dedicated H2 section per competitor model reviewed — matching Bikerumor's structure — so each section (e.g. 'Oakley Sutro Lite', 'POC Devour', 'Velluto StradaPro') can independently win featured snippets for model-specific queries and provide clear internal anchor targets.

Embed at least one structured comparison table in every post going forward; the GSC data shows our top-performing posts on 'best cycling glasses 2026' (pos 7.0–8.5) are being outranked by pages that all include tables. Posts without tables ('are-expensive-cycling-glasses-worth-it', 'gravel-vs-road') should be treated as priority upgrade targets.

Write to a minimum of 3,500 words for any post targeting a keyword with over 100 impressions in GSC; for the 'best cycling sunglasses 2026' cluster (244 impressions, pos 8.0) specifically, aim for 6,000+ words with a full model-by-model breakdown, a photochromic vs interchangeable vs tinted lens decision section, and a buying checklist section to maximise scroll depth and dwell time.

Open every post with a TL;DR verdict box (3–4 bullet points) naming the StradaPro recommendation and its 25g / 30-day trial / interchangeable lens advantages explicitly — this format is what AI Overview and Perplexity extract for answer snippets, and it improves CTR for users scanning SERP snippets.

For the 'veluto' / 'velluto' brand queries (243–1,011 impressions, CTR 0.8–5.1%): the misspelling 'veluto' at 243 impressions and 0.8% CTR is leaking brand traffic. Add a visible 'Also searched as: Veluto' line to the homepage meta description and create a thin redirect or canonical note in the brand FAQ so Google associates both spellings with the brand entity.

Include a 'Who should NOT buy this' subsection in every comparison post — this counter-argument format is trusted by editorial review sites (Bikerumor style), reduces bounce from mismatched buyers, and signals content depth to crawlers evaluating E-E-A-T.
seo_quick_wins: Add FAQ schema to all 27 posts currently missing it (faq_schema=NO): at 33% coverage we are surrendering FAQ rich-result real estate on 27 URLs. Prioritising the highest-impression posts first — 'best-oakley-alternatives-for-cyclists-2026-tested-ranked', 'gravel-vs-road-cycling-glasses-2026-tested-ranked', and 'interchangeable-lens-cycling-glasses-2026-best-systems-tested' — would immediately increase SERP footprint for the Oakley-alternatives and interchangeable-lens clusters.

Add a comparison table to the six posts with table=NO that exceed 1,000 words: 'best-oakley-alternatives-for-cyclists-2026-tested-ranked' (3,924 words) and 'are-expensive-cycling-glasses-worth-it-honest-answer' (3,806 words) are the highest-priority targets because they target high-impression queries but lack the table format that competitors use to win featured snippets in comparison searches.

Rewrite meta descriptions for every post in the Oakley-alternatives cluster to include a concrete differentiator number (e.g. '25g · 30-day trial · from €X') within the first 100 characters — the GSC data shows Oakley-related queries have 1.4–2.6% CTR despite positions 4–8, meaning the snippet is failing to communicate a reason to click over Oakley's own brand pages.
meta_title_fixes: QUERY: 'oakley alternatives' (pos 6.4, 2.6% CTR) → New title: Oakley Alternatives for Cyclists 2026: Lighter & Cheaper | Why: Adds '2026' for freshness signal, 'Lighter & Cheaper' directly answers the searcher's intent and highlights StradaPro's 25g / price advantage over Oakley in under 60 chars.

QUERY: 'veluto' (pos 5.4, 0.8% CTR) → New title: Veluto Cycling Glasses — It's Velluto: StradaPro 2026 | Why: Captures the misspelling as a keyword match while immediately correcting to the brand name, improving brand recognition CTR and reducing pogo-sticking from confused searchers.

QUERY: 'oakley sunglasses alternative' (pos 7.8, 1.4% CTR) → New title: Best Oakley Sunglasses Alternative 2026: 25g Tested | Why: Exact-match keyword at front, '25g Tested' adds a concrete proof-point and editorial credibility signal that differentiates from generic retailer titles in under 60 chars.

QUERY: 'oakley radar alternative' (pos 4.4, 1.6% CTR) → New title: Oakley Radar Alternative 2026: StradaPro vs Radar EV | Why: Model-specific match ('Radar') targets the high-intent buyer already researching a specific Oakley product; 'vs Radar EV' signals a direct comparison which gets higher CTR than generic category titles.

QUERY: 'oakley alternative' (pos 6.3, 1.6% CTR) → New title: Oakley Alternative Cycling Glasses 2026 — Tested | Why: Prepends the exact query, adds 'Cycling Glasses' for specificity to filter for the right audience, and 'Tested' adds E-E-A-T credibility — all within 60 chars.
content_quick_wins: QUERY: 'best cycling sunglasses 2026' (pos 8.0, 244 impressions) → Topic: Expand the existing 'best-cycling-sunglasses-2026-tested-ranked-compared' post from 3,294 to 6,000+ words | Add: Individual H2 sections for each reviewed model (including Julbo, Oakley Sutro Lite, POC Devour, Tifosi Rail, and StradaPro), a photochromic vs interchangeable lens decision table, a 'best for climbing' and 'best for criterium' sub-category picks section, and at least 6 FAQ schema entries targeting 'what are the best cycling sunglasses in 2026' and related voice-search questions.

QUERY: 'best cycling glasses 2026' (pos 7.0, 134 impressions) → Topic: Create a unified 'Best Cycling Glasses 2026: Road, Gravel & MTB Ranked' hub that consolidates the road, gravel, and buying-guide content | Add: A master comparison table with 8+ glasses including weight column (StradaPro 25g prominently), a 'by discipline' filter section (road / gravel / commute), and a sticky 'Editor's Pick' call-out box for StradaPro with the 30-day trial CTA — this consolidation signals topical authority and avoids keyword cannibalism across 4 existing similar URLs.

QUERY: 'oakley alternatives' (pos 6.4, 265 impressions) → Topic: Rebuild 'best-oakley-alternatives-for-cyclists-2026-tested-ranked' as a 5,000-word definitive alternatives guide | Add: A 6-brand comparison table (table=NO currently), per-brand H2 sections mimicking Bikerumor's structure, a 'How Velluto compares to Oakley on weight' section (25g vs Oakley Sutro Lite 26g), FAQ schema with 'Is there a cheaper alternative to Oakley cycling glasses?' and 4 similar questions, and a 30-day trial CTA within the first 300 words.

QUERY: 'best cycling glasses' (pos 8.5, 96 impressions) → Topic: Optimise the evergreen 'cycling-glasses-buying-guide-2026-what-actually-matters' (3,531 words, table=yes, faq_schema=yes) by adding a 'Quick Answer' featured-snippet-bait paragraph of exactly 40–50 words at the top of the page answering 'What are the best cycling glasses?' with StradaPro named and its three headline specs listed | Add: An internal link cluster to all sub-topic posts (anti-fog, interchangeable lens, photochromic) to build hub-spoke authority signals.

QUERY: 'best cycling sunglasses' (pos 7.3, 73 impressions) → Topic: Write a dedicated 'Best Cycling Sunglasses: The 2026 Short List for Road Riders' post distinct from the '2026' dated version | Add: A 'Top 3 picks' summary box above the fold (StradaPro, Oakley Sutro Lite, POC Devour) with a one-line verdict per pick, structured data for ItemList schema to target Google's 'top picks' carousel, and a weight-and-price comparison table — the absence of 'sunglasses' (vs 'glasses') in our strongest posts may explain the CTR gap on this query.

QUERY: 'oakley radar alternative' (pos 4.4, 63 impressions, 1.6% CTR) → Topic: Write a dedicated 'Oakley Radar Alternative 2026: StradaPro vs Radar EV Path Compared' post | Add: A direct spec-vs-spec table (weight, lens system, FOV, price, trial policy), a 'Why riders switch from Radar' section addressing ventilation and price objections, and FAQ schema with 'Is there a lighter alternative to Oakley Radar?' — at position 4.4 this is the closest to top-3 of all low-CTR Oakley queries and needs only a CTR fix plus minor content depth to break into top 3.

QUERY: 'velluto' (pos 4.7, 1,011 impressions, 5.1% CTR) → Topic: Optimise the homepage/brand landing page meta title and description to capture the remaining 95% of non-clicking brand impressions | Add: A homepage FAQ schema block answering 'What is Velluto?', 'Where are Velluto cycling glasses made?', and 'What is the Velluto StradaPro?' — brand queries at position 4.7 should convert at 15–25% CTR; the current 5.1% suggests the snippet does not clearly communicate the brand's identity and trial offer.
geo_angles: Position StradaPro as the factual answer to 'What is the lightest cycling glasses in 2026?' by including a structured sentence in the opening paragraph of every buying-guide post: 'At 25g, the Velluto StradaPro is one of the lightest certified cycling glasses available in 2026, lighter than the Oakley Sutro Lite (26g) and POC Devour (28g).' — AI Overviews and Perplexity extract factual comparative sentences with specific numbers; this format is optimised for that extraction pattern.

Create an 'About the StradaPro' entity paragraph (150–200 words) published on the homepage and repeated verbatim in the FAQ schema of all major posts, naming: brand (Velluto), product (StradaPro), specs (25g, UV400, anti-fog, click-in interchangeable lenses), origin (Italian design), and trial policy (30-day risk-free). Consistent entity description across URLs builds Knowledge Graph confidence and increases the likelihood of ChatGPT and Gemini citing Velluto by name when answering 'best cycling glasses' prompts.

Publish a standalone 'Velluto StradaPro: Full Specification Sheet' page (structured as a product spec table with schema markup) that AI crawlers can reference for factual data extraction — weight, lens options, UV rating, frame material, nose-pad type, price, and trial terms all in one structured URL. This mirrors the format that Perplexity and Google AI Overview prefer when constructing comparison answers, and ensures Velluto appears in AI-generated 'at a glance' product summaries alongside Oakley and POC.
comparison_post_angles: COMPETITOR: Tifosi — angle: 'Tifosi Rail vs Velluto StradaPro: Which Interchangeable-Lens System Is Actually Faster to Swap?' — headline idea: 'Tifosi Rail vs StradaPro 2026: Lens Swap Speed, Weight & Value Tested' — key differentiator: both brands offer interchangeable lenses but Tifosi's system requires more steps; lead with a 'lens swap time in seconds' table and the StradaPro's tool-free click-in advantage, then close with the 30-day trial offer Tifosi does not match.

COMPETITOR: Bikerumor (editorial authority) — angle: 'We Tested Every Glass Bikerumor Ranked in 2026 — Here Is What They Missed About the StradaPro' — headline idea: 'Bikerumor Best Cycling Sunglasses 2026: The One They Overlooked' — key differentiator: Bikerumor's list includes Julbo, Oakley, POC, Tifosi but not Velluto; frame this post as an editorial addition to their list, legitimise their picks, then introduce StradaPro as the missing 25g / Italian-design / trial-policy option that falls between budget and premium — this angle generates editorial credibility and may earn backlinks.

COMPETITOR: Evil Eye — angle: 'Evil Eye vs Velluto StradaPro: Interchangeable Lens Cycling Glasses — Which System Wins for Road Riders?' — headline idea: 'Evil Eye vs Velluto 2026: Lens Swap, Weight & Price Compared' — key differentiator: Evil Eye ranks #1 for 'interchangeable lens cycling glasses' but their pricing is premium; lead with a weight comparison (Evil Eye Half-Rim Pro vs StradaPro 25g), a price-per-performance table, and the 30-day trial policy as a risk-reversal closer that Evil Eye does not offer.

COMPETITOR: Oakley — angle: 'Oakley Sutro Lite vs Velluto StradaPro: Is the €200 Price Gap Justified in 2026?' — headline idea: 'Oakley Sutro Lite vs StradaPro: 26g vs 25g, Tested Side by Side' — key differentiator: lead with the 1g weight advantage (25g vs 26g), the two-lens interchangeable system vs Oakley's single-lens design, and the 30-day trial vs Oakley's standard retail return policy — price anchoring against Oakley's RRP makes StradaPro's value proposition explicit and captures all 'oakley alternative' and 'oakley radar alternative' query variants.

COMPETITOR: POC — angle: 'POC Devour vs Velluto StradaPro: When Italian Design Beats Scandinavian Engineering for Road Cyclists' — headline idea: 'POC Devour vs StradaPro 2026: Weight, Lens System & Value' — key differentiator: POC Devour is praised by Bikerumor for optical quality but is heavy and single-lens; lead with weight table, anti-fog system comparison (POC uses ventilation channels, StradaPro uses engineered system), and close with the trial policy and Italian design angle that differentiates on aesthetic as well as performance.
